theme: Distribute engines across drop-away rocket stages
title: Drop-Away Booster Staging — loss-aware engine and propellant allocation
strategies: trivial: one engine per stage, equal propellant split -> reproduces the grader baseline (~0.1)

greedy: classic loss-free geometric staging (equal mass ratio per stage) with a fixed uniform engine count of 2, blind to the loss table L_i -> it piles the most propellant onto the high-L bottom stage and lets it burn long, so on the spiked-loss traps it wastes delta-v

strong: loss-aware joint coordinate ascent over integer engine counts and continuous propellant split; on a bottom-spike it shortens that burn (adds engines and pulls propellant off the spiked stage) and reallocates freed mass up the stack where the downstream jettison cascade pays for it
alternatives: continuous relaxation + rounding, per-stage marginal delta-v/loss balancing, or a fuller mixed-integer search over engine counts
